title: Automated Client Reporting for Real Estate
metaDescription: Done-for-you recurring client reports for real estate firms. Listings, leads, and deal progress sent on a schedule, in your voice, without manual prep.
excerpt: Recurring client reports for real estate teams, built on the Cadence layer of your AI Operating System. Reports go out on schedule, in your voice, every time.

sections.intro:
If you sell or manage property, your sellers, landlords, and investor clients all want the same thing: regular updates. They want to know how many viewings the listing got this week, where the leads came from, what offers are on the table, and what you are doing next. Most agents end up writing these updates by hand on Friday afternoon, or worse, on Sunday night. Some weeks the reports go out. Some weeks they slip. Clients notice the slip more than the report.

The Ayothedoc AI Operating System (AIOS) installs Automated Client Reporting as a recurring capability. Your system pulls live data from your CRM, listing portals, inbox, and calendar, drafts the report in your voice, and sends it on the cadence you and your client agreed to. You review and approve, or let it send on its own once you trust it. You stay the agent. The reporting stops being a task.
sections.fourCFit:
Automated Client Reporting sits in the Cadence layer of your AIOS. Cadence is the layer that makes work happen on a schedule, without anyone asking. It works alongside the other three layers:

  • Context: the system knows your firm, your listings, your clients, and how you write to them.
  • Connections: it reads from the CRM, listing platforms, inbox, calendar, and any portal analytics you already use.
  • Capabilities: it drafts the report, summarizes activity, and flags anything that needs your attention.
  • Cadence: it runs the report weekly, fortnightly, or monthly, per client, and delivers it on time.

The same Cadence layer can also run your internal pipeline reviews, end-of-month commission summaries, and landlord statements once this first report is in place.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
• A scheduled client report per active listing or managed property, sent on the day and time you choose.
  • Content includes: new enquiries this period, viewings booked and held, viewer feedback themes, portal impressions and clicks, offers received, price and market notes, and next steps.
  • Written in your voice using a tone profile we build from your past emails.
  • Delivered by email, with an optional PDF attachment branded to your firm.
  • Owner SLA: each report is drafted and queued at least 12 hours before send time, so you have a review window.
  • Send SLA: reports go out within the scheduled hour, with a backup channel if a data source is down.
  • A simple dashboard listing every client, their cadence, and the last and next report date.
sections.howWeInstall:
1. Audit (free, about 10 minutes): we map your current reporting habits, client list, data sources, and the cadence each client expects. Run the audit.
  2. Install (10 business days): we connect your CRM, listing data, and inbox, build the report template and voice profile, set the schedule per client, and run a parallel week so you can compare drafts to what you would have written.
  3. Operate (ongoing): reports run on schedule, we monitor delivery, and we ship one new automation per week. That might be vendor weekly updates first, then landlord monthly statements, then investor quarterly summaries. See plans.
sections.expectedResults:
• Reporting time per client drops from 20 to 40 minutes per cycle to a 2 to 5 minute review.
  • Across a book of 20 to 40 active clients, that is typically 10 to 25 hours back per month for the lead agent.
  • Client retention improves because reports actually arrive, on time, every cycle.
  • Fewer "any update?" emails from sellers and landlords, because the update already landed.
  • Standing guarantee: across your full AIOS install, we help you recover 40 or more hours a month, or we keep working free until you do.
sections.faq:
Will my clients know the reports are AI-assisted?

The reports are written in your voice, from your data, and reviewed by you before they send. They read like reports you wrote. We do not hide the system, but it is not labeled as automated either. It is your reporting, running on your AIOS.

What if a listing has a quiet week with nothing to say?

The report still sends, because silence is the thing clients worry about. It states the activity honestly, notes market context, and recommends the next action, whether that is a price review, a fresh photo set, or a new portal push. You approve the recommendation before it goes out.

Can different clients get different cadences and formats?

Yes. Vendors often want weekly during an active campaign. Landlords usually want monthly. Investors may want quarterly with a portfolio roll-up. Each client gets their own schedule, template, and delivery channel, all managed from one dashboard.
